nodes.label: Stargate - Bash Command Classifier for AI Coding Agents
Classification Levels (RED/GREEN/YELLOW)
Precedent Corpus - SQLite database of past classification decisions
Feedback Loop - post-tool-use hook records outcomes
Scope-Based Trust - operator-defined scopes in stargate.toml
Shell AST Parsing via mvdan.cc/sh/v3
LLM Review for YELLOW commands (Claude)
Secret Scrubbing - redact secrets before LLM/corpus
Command Cache for repeated YELLOW decisions
LLM File Retrieval with path validation
stargate.toml - root trust anchor config file
Claude Code Integration via pre/post tool-use hooks
Localhost-Only Binding (127.0.0.1)
Fail-Closed Design - block when server unreachable
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY - direct API authentication
CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN - subprocess authentication via claude CLI
internal/rules - Rule compilation and matching
internal/classifier - Pipeline orchestration (parse -> rules -> corpus -> LLM)
internal/corpus - SQLite precedent corpus, similarity search
internal/llm - Provider interface, Anthropic implementation, prompt templating
internal/server - HTTP handlers (/classify, /feedback, /health, /test)
internal/parser - Shell parsing, AST walking, CommandInfo extraction
internal/scopes - Scope definitions, resolver interface
cmd/stargate - CLI entry point, subcommand dispatch
Security Framework - Four Defense Layers
AST Parsing Layer - mvdan.cc/sh/v3 Shell Parser
Rule Engine Layer - TOML-Defined RED/GREEN/YELLOW Rules
Contextual Trust Layer - Scopes and Resolvers
LLM Review Layer - Provider-Agnostic Classification
Defense-in-Depth Rationale - No Single Layer Sufficient
Trust Boundaries - stargate.toml as Root Trust Anchor
Panel Review Process - Synthetic Expert Panel
Milestone Transition Protocol - Design Verification
Security Design Checklist - 9 Required Items
Dependency: mvdan.cc/sh/v3 - Shell Parser and AST
Dependency: anthropic-sdk-go - Anthropic/Claude LLM Provider
Dependency: BurntSushi/toml - TOML Config Parsing
Dependency: modernc.org/sqlite - Pure-Go SQLite
Dependency: go.opentelemetry.io/otel - OpenTelemetry SDK
Yellow Commands Test Data - Ambiguous Command Samples
Red Commands Test Data - Dangerous Command Samples
Evasion Commands Test Data - Obfuscation Vectors
Green Commands Test Data - Safe Command Samples
Accepted Risk: TOCTOU in File Retrieval Path Validation
Accepted Risk: Base64-Encoded Secrets Not Detected by Scrubbing
Accepted Risk: Variable Names May Reveal Secret Existence
Accepted Risk: files_requested Reveals LLM Reasoning Patterns
Accepted Risk: Precedent Reasoning Accumulation
Accepted Risk: Adversarial Instructions in Corpus Reasoning
Accepted Risk: --allow-remote Without Transport Security
Accepted Risk: tool_name Bypass via Agent Renaming
Accepted Risk: Telemetry Env Var Overrides Bypass stargate.toml
Accepted Risk: POST /test as Classification Oracle
Accepted Risk: Unscrubbed llm_raw_response in /test Debug Output
Accepted Risk: RuleSnapshot in /test Debug Trace Entries
Accepted Risk: ScopePatterns in /test Debug Resolve Detail
Accepted Risk: rendered_prompts in Default /test Debug Output
Rationale: TOCTOU not privilege escalation since attacker already has local access
Rationale: Base64 detection too error-prone; LLM is classification tool not exfiltration channel
Rationale: Balanced polarity injection, truncation, TTL decay bound adversarial corpus impact
Rationale: --allow-remote is explicit operator opt-in, not default
Rationale: Localhost access is trust boundary; /test emits telemetry for audit
Stargate Implementation Plan
nodes.author: Derek Perez

nodes.label: M0: Skeleton - CLI, Config, HTTP Server, /health
M1: Parser + Walker - AST Parsing and CommandInfo Extraction
M1 Retrospective - 84 Threads, 20 Rounds, Underspecified Design
M2: Rule Engine - RED/GREEN/YELLOW Matching, /classify Endpoint
M2 Retrospective - 61 Threads, API Schema and Handler Hardening
M3: Scopes + Resolvers - Contextual Trust Matching
M3 Retrospective - 28 Threads, Panel Review Effective
M4: LLM Review - Provider Interface, Prompts, Scrubbing
M4 Retrospective - 91 Threads, Split-PR Amplification
Debug & Observability Implementation Plan
Task 1: Rule Trace Types (RuleTraceEntry, RuleSnapshot, ResolveDebug)
Task 2: Rule Engine Trace Support (EvaluateWithTrace)
Task 3: Debug Types in Classifier (DebugInfo)
Task 4: LLM Raw Response Surfacing (RawBody on ReviewResponse)
Task 5: Classifier Debug Population (DryRun=true wiring)
Task 6: /test Handler Debug Serialization (wrapper struct)
Task 7: Corpus Recent() Query
Task 8: corpus recent CLI Command
Task 9: stargate explain CLI Command
Task 10: Red Team Condition - File Retrieval Path Validation Test
Task 11: Integration Test and Final Verification
Debug & Observability Design Spec
Problem: No visibility into LLM inputs, rule traces, time-based queries, or formatted output
Design: /test Debug Enrichment (DebugInfo with json:"-" tag)
Design: Rule Engine Trace (EvaluateWithTrace with per-invocation evalContext)
Design: corpus recent CLI Command
Design: stargate explain CLI Command
Security Considerations for Debug Observability
Rationale: json:"-" tag on Debug field prevents /classify exposure
Rationale: Per-invocation evalContext avoids Engine struct mutation for concurrency safety
Rationale: Unscrubbed llm_raw_response maximizes diagnostic value
Rationale: recover() guard prevents debug panics from affecting classification
Rationale: Default rule trace filtering reduces noise from ~83 to ~5-10 rules
Rationale: No new HTTP endpoints - reuse existing /test for debug data
internal/rules/trace.go - RuleTraceEntry, RuleSnapshot, ResolveDebug types
internal/classifier/debug.go - DebugInfo, CacheDebug, PrecedentDebug, PromptDebug
cmd/stargate/explain.go - stargate explain subcommand
DryRun mode - /test endpoint classification without corpus/feedback writes
evalContext - per-invocation trace state, stack-local, never on Engine struct
Wrapper struct for /test debug serialization (testResponse embeds ClassifyResponse + Debug)
8 matching steps in matchRule: command, subcommands, flags, args, scope, context, resolve, pattern
Debugging workflow: SSH to fly.io VMs, pull corpus + config, iterate with /test locally
Stargate Design Specification (PRD v0.2.0)

nodes.label: Architecture - HTTP Server + Shell Parser + Rule Engine + LLM
Rationale: Go Language Choice - mvdan.cc/sh, Static Binary, Fast Startup
Shell Parser: mvdan.cc/sh/v3/syntax - AST Node Coverage
LLM Provider Interface - ReviewerProvider with ReviewRequest
Anthropic Authentication - API Key vs OAuth Token
Rationale: TOML Config Format - Comments, Unambiguous Types
Scopes and Resolvers - Contextual Trust Design
github_repo_owner Resolver - --repo, gh api path, .git/config
url_domain Resolver - Domain Extraction from URL Arguments
Scope Injection into LLM Prompts - Trust Boundary Context
Rationale: Liberal GitHub Classification - PAT Token as Permission Boundary
POST /classify API - Agent-Agnostic Classification Endpoint
POST /feedback API - Post-Execution Outcome Recording
Feedback Token Protocol - HMAC-SHA256 Authentication
Agent Adapters - stargate hook Protocol Translation
Claude Code Adapter - PreToolUse/PostToolUse Hook Integration
Classification Pipeline - 10-Stage Parse to Decision Flow
AST Walking Strategy - CommandInfo Extraction from Syntax Tree
Prefix Stripping Design - Wrapper Command Resolution
Rule Matching Logic - Field Matching, Flag Normalization, Evaluation Order
LLM Review Protocol - Two-Call File Retrieval with Scrubbing
Secret Scrubbing Pipeline - Env Vars, Tokens, URL Credentials
XML Fence Escaping - Prompt Injection Defense via Tag Stripping
Structural Signatures - Argument-Agnostic Command Fingerprints
Similarity Scoring - Jaccard Index of Signature Tuples
Corpus Integrity - Rate Limits, Balanced Injection, TTL Decay
Telemetry - OpenTelemetry Traces, Metrics, Logs to Grafana Cloud
Evasion Vectors and Mitigations - 30+ Techniques Addressed
Fail-Closed Design - Parse Error, Timeout, Resolver Failure Handling
Command Cache - In-Memory SHA-256 Deduplication
LLM System Prompt Template - Security Classifier Instructions
Configuration File Specification (stargate.toml)
Default RED Rules - Destructive, Privilege Escalation, Exfiltration
Default GREEN Rules - Read-Only, Toolchains, Trusted Scopes
Default YELLOW Rules - Network, Docker, Package Install, Shell
Design Decision: Hot-Reload Removed - HMAC Secret Complexity
